{{Quote|The strongest stars have hearts of kyber.|[[Chirrut Îmwe]]|<ref name="Rogue One" />}}
A '''kyber crystal''', simply known as a '''kyber''' and described as a '''lightsaber crystal''' or the '''living crystal''', was a rare, [[The Force|Force]]-attuned [[crystal]] that grew naturally and was found on various [[planet]]s across [[the galaxy]]. Known in ancient times as a '''kaiburr crystal''', it was used by both the [[Jedi]] and the [[Sith]] in the construction of their [[lightsaber]]s. As part of their [[Jedi training]], [[Jedi Initiate|younglings]] were sent to the [[Crystal Cave]] of the [[Water|ice]] planet of [[Ilum]] to seek their own lightsaber crystals. The crystal's mix of unique luster was called "the water of the kyber" by the Jedi.<ref name="Catalyst" /> There were also larger, rarer crystals of great power and that, according to legends, were used at the heart of ancient [[superweapon]]s by the Sith.

Through an unspecified process involving being placed in a furnace and hammering, kyber crystals could be tempered to match with the nature of multiple individuals multiple times, beside bonding with just one for life. When these tempered crystals are placed within a weapon such as a lightsaber, its color will change within moments upon being picked up by a new wielder.

==Description==
{{Quote|Kyber crystals are rare gems capable of focusing and amplifying energy. They can be found on planets that have a high natural concentration of Force energy, such as Jedha and Ilum.|[[Luke Skywalker]]|<ref name="Secrets of the Jedi">''[[Star Wars: The Secrets of the Jedi]]''</ref>}}
Kyber [[crystal]]s were [[The Force|Force]]-attuned crystals that grew rarely but throughout [[the galaxy]], with some areas having a greater abundance than others. The crystals concentrated energy in a unique manner through the Force, resonating with it.<ref name="Ultimate Star Wars">''[[Ultimate Star Wars]]''</ref> The crystals exhibited a sort of "collective consciousness," verging on [[sentience]], and could non-verbally communicate with both one another and living beings.<ref name="Ahsoka">[[Ahsoka (novel)|''Ahsoka'']]</ref> Some theorized that the permutations in the kyber lattice were comparable to emotional shifts in a sentient being. According to superstition, the kyber's secrets could only be unlocked by facing in a certain direction at dawn or by watching mist rise on specific remote worlds. A Jedi once called the kyber a somnolent stone that needed to be woken up to be used, requiring care to not insult the crystal.<ref name="Catalyst" />

[[File:Jyn_kyber_pendant.png|thumb|left|100px|An uncolored kyber crystal used as the pendant of a [[Jyn's kyber pendant|necklace]]]]
Internally, kyber crystals were composed of both organic and inorganic matter. They were stable at [[temperature]]s and pressures found in the cores of large [[star]]s, and were thus impervious to [[fire]] and resistant to hammering, and noted for their intricate patterns. Kyber crystals were sturdy enough to survive the violent explosion of a [[lightsaber]]'s power grid, such as those caused by accidentally inverting the emitter matrix during the weapon's assembly.<ref name="A Test of Strength">{{TCW|A Test of Strength}}</ref> Kyber crystals would not warm a sheath or a towel or any inanimate object, responding only to life, including [[plant]] life. If an individual were to wrap their hand around a crystal, the crystal would warm, but not change in temperature.<ref name="Catalyst" /> Many scientists believed that kyber crystals could shift their shape depending on ambient energies.<ref name="MtD" /> The crystals grew in an organized way, adding to their prismatic structure one piece at a time. They had no discernible lifespan, and thus could be found from sizes as small as a fingernail to gigantic proportions. All kybers were attuned to the Force regardless of their size.<ref name="Ahsoka" /> Incorrectly pumping laser energy into the crystals weakened their lattices in the same way living cells were affected by radiation.<ref name="Catalyst" />

True kybers were only found in veins of pure [[kyberite]]. If the crystal contained impurities, it was regarded as a false kyber. [[Ranite]] was closely related to kyberite, but was denser and tougher, and did not contain kyber crystals. Crystals were typically brought to the surface of planets by movements along fault lines, exhumed from the high temperatures and pressure conditions in which they formed. The crystals could be mined from secondary deposits in streams, perhaps following long transport, owing to their mechanical and chemical resilience. Alternately, they could be found in their primary, ''in situ'' positions, having grown into free space on the walls of lava tubes, caves, fault zones, and within veins of kyberite.<ref name="Catalyst" />

[[File:Ganodi-Crystal_Caves.png|thumb|right|240px|The Crystal Cave of Ilum]]
Kyber crystals were most notably found in [[Ilum]]'s [[Crystal Cave]]. They were a critical component in the construction of lightsabers, focusing energy into the weapon's distinct [[plasma]] blade. The crystals shared a special relationship with the [[Jedi]] for tens of thousands of [[Standard year|years]], and welcomed them to hear their song and learn their lessons. [[Jedi Initiate|Jedi younglings]] traveled to the ice caves to harvest their own crystals, which they then used to build their lightsabers. The Force guided their selection, finding a crystal that matched them as Jedi. To those not meant to receive it, the crystal was cold to the touch, but those the crystal chose would hear its "[[music]]" and "harmony," becoming attracted to it.<ref name="Ahsoka" /> Crystals lacked [[color]] before they were chosen by a Jedi. Once chosen, most lightsabers became blue or green, though other shades were created in some instances, most notably [[Mace Windu]]'s [[Mace Windu's lightsaber|purple blade]], the [[Lightsaber pike|yellow blades]] of the [[Jedi Temple Guard]]s, the black [[Darksaber]], and [[Baylan Skoll]]'s [[Baylan Skoll's lightsaber|orange lightsaber]].<ref name="Databank" /><ref name="Ahsoka" /><ref name="The Wrong Jedi">{{TCW|The Wrong Jedi}}</ref><ref name="Part One">{{Ahsoka|Part One: Master and Apprentice}}</ref> The resulting crystal and blade color was decided through shifting to match the nature of the [[Force-bond#Application|bonded]] owner.<ref name="Secrets of the Jedi">''[[Star Wars: The Secrets of the Jedi]]''</ref> It was possible to alter the angle or frequency of kyber crystals or lightsaber blades, which allowed the colors of blades to be changed. This was done by [[Jedi Knight]] [[Anakin Skywalker]] to his former [[Padawan]]'s [[Ahsoka Tano's lightsabers|lightsabers]].<ref name="CWD:TPA">{{SWYouTube|series=Clone Wars Download|text="The Phantom Apprentice"|video=d_NmvaJyzj0|sw_url=video/clone-wars-download-the-phantom-apprentice}}</ref>

[[File:KyloRenLightsaber-TFA.png|thumb|left|140px|Diagram of Kylo Ren's lightsaber displaying its kyber crystal, cracked during its bleeding]]
Kyber crystals were inherently filled with "[[Light side of the Force|light side]] energy," and resisted any effort by [[Dark side of the Force|dark-side]] practitioners to corrupt them, with one crystal in particular, that belonged to the [[Jedi Master]] [[Kirak Infil'a]], subjecting Darth Vader to intense visions after he attempted to corrupt the crystal using the dark-side of the Force. This process caused the crystal to "[[Bleeding|bleed]]," as if it were a living organism, resulting in the distinctive red or crimson-bladed lightsabers synonymous with the Sith.<ref name="Dark Lord of the Sith 5">[[Darth Vader (2017) 5|''Darth Vader'' (2017) 5]]</ref> It was possible to "heal" a corrupted kyber crystal. The information on how to heal a kyber crystal was found in the [[Chronicles of Brus-bu|Chronicles of Brus-Bu]].<ref name="TROSVD" /> One instance of purifying a corrupted kyber crystal was when Ahsoka Tano purified the two crystals she retrieved from the [[Sixth Brother]]'s [[Double-bladed spinning lightsaber|lightsaber]] following his defeat. In this case, the crystals turned white.<ref name="Ahsoka" />

The crystals were noted for their unmatched ability to channel energy and generate enormous returns. To this end, numerous individuals attempted to make [[synthetic kyber crystal]]s, though their artificial nature made them highly unstable and explosive.<ref name="Uprising">''[[Star Wars: Uprising]]''</ref> If a crystal was subjected to forceful attempts to study it or harness its power, it would "flee" such attempts, hindering those that sought to use it against its will. Furthermore, those that worked in proximity to them would experience headaches and a loss of [[sleep]], becoming incessantly fanatic and fixated over the crystal in the process.<ref name="Catalyst" />

[[File:Broken_saber.png|thumb|right|230px|The Skywalker lightsaber was damaged by an explosion of energy that broke and exposed the crystal within.]]
For [[Standard century|centuries]], the Jedi cut and faceted the kybers to eliminate occlusions and intensify their power yield. [[Galen Walton Erso|Galen Erso]] theorized that larger crystals needed to be faceted, not only to eliminate these occlusions, but also to minimize diffraction resulting from the introduction of energy from a lasing medium. With proper faceting, the pulse of energy released by the crystal could be greatly amplified and, with the help of containment devices, or be directed into a collimating beam of incredible power, as was used on the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|DS-1]] [[battle station]]. Galen Erso also theorized that increased energy yield could be further attained by forcing what the Jedi called the "day and night" lattices of the Force to realign. By realigning the crystals' lattices along the dark or nighttime axis of the Force, Galen postulated that it could allow for greater control over the crystal's tendency to diffract. Thus, technology could be used to force the crystals to obey their masters. The abundant yield could then be siphoned off, contained, and used as enriched energy. Without containment, the yield could result in a catastrophic event, capable of wiping out a large population center.<ref name="Catalyst" />

Specifically, when one giant kyber crystal was [[Mission to Faos Station|destabilized]] by [[Saw Gerrera]], it absorbed so much energy that when it finally did reach critical mass, it exploded with enough force to destroy both the [[freighter]] [[Freighter 2716|transporting]] it and a nearby [[Slavin's Star Destroyer|Star Destroyer]].<ref name="In the Name of the Rebellion">{{Rebels|In the Name of the Rebellion}}</ref>

Kyber crystals could be used to power other weapons than lightsabers. People had tried to make kyber [[bomb]]s and kyber [[blaster]]s, and there were a few weapons of that kind in the [[Jedi Archives]].<ref name="Lesson">{{StoryCite|story=The Lesson (Obi-Wan & Anakin)|book=Star Wars (2020) 25|sformatted=The Lesson (Obi-Wan & Anakin)}}</ref> They were also used in [[kyber mirror]]s.<ref name="TBoJ">''[[The High Republic: The Battle of Jedha]]''</ref>

==History==
===Sacred crystal of the Jedi===
{{Quote|Long ago in forgotten times, when the Sith and Jedi fought for control of the galaxy, weapons there were, of unimaginable power. Always at their heart, a kyber crystal was.|Yoda|<ref name="Big Bang">{{TCW|The Big Bang}}</ref>}}
[[File:KyberJediStatue-GalaxysEdge3.png|thumb|left|200px|Kyber statue on Jedha]]
Before the [[Jedi Order|Jedi]] and Sith orders, kyber crystals were worshiped by primitive societies for their fire-resistant properties, intricate structures, and near impermeability, signs which made them appear outwardly eternal. Ancient beings associated them with wind, [[Water|rain]], and breath. It is believed that the Jedi saw them as embodying an aspect of the Force. Over the centuries, the Jedi amassed an almost exclusive right to the crystals, protecting and collecting them to decorate their temples and power their lightsabers. Some saw this collection of the kybers as proof of the Jedi's inordinate greed, and their secrecy over its nature a result of their wish to remain in power. Others saw the Jedi as protecting the [[galaxy]] from those who might use the powers of the crystal for dark purposes.<ref name="Catalyst" />

Within the [[Temple of the Kyber]] on [[Jedha]], the Jedi were able to sculpt some 2,304 statues out of kyber using lightsabers. These statues lined the tunnels of the temple and were guarded by the [[Guardians of the Whills]].<ref name="Galaxy's Edge 3">[[Galaxy's Edge 3|''Galaxy's Edge'' 3]]</ref> Another Kyber structure was the [[Kyber Arch]] located within one of the meditation chambers of the [[Imperial Palace|Jedi Temple]] on the [[Core Worlds|Core World]] of [[Coruscant]]. The Arch was built out of individual Kyber crystals retrieved from the lightsabers of Jedi who had fallen in battle, becoming an advanced meditation practice that honored their sacrifice. Jedi Master [[Jora Malli]] once questioned her Padawan [[Reath Silas]] why a single Jedi could never cross the Arch alone. Silas came to realize the question was not to be taken literally, as she wanted him to see how, in order for a single Jedi to cross, the rest of the Order had needed to build the Arch and others had needed to fall in battle.<ref name="Into the Dark">''[[The High Republic: Into the Dark]]''</ref>

[[File:Crystal_Crisis_Ep.png|thumb|right|250px|Anakin Skywalker and Obi-Wan Kenobi with a giant kyber crystal on Utapau]]
During ancient wars between the Jedi and the Sith, in which both sides fought for control over the galaxy, [[superweapon]]s were created by the Sith<ref name="Death Star Superlaser">{{Databank|death-star-superlaser|Death Star Superlaser}}</ref> that were powered by giant kyber crystals.<ref name="Big Bang" /> Around [[3966 BBY]]<ref name="Date">''[[Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker: The Visual Dictionary]]'' states that [[Sith]] accounts describing the [[Malachor superweapon]] were made four-thousand [[Standard year|years]] before the [[Hosnian Cataclysm|Starkiller Incident]], which ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' dates to [[34 ABY]]. From this it can deduced that the accounts were made in 3966 BBY.</ref> the [[Sith Lord]] [[Darth Tanis]] developed kyber weaponry for the Sith on [[Malachor]].<ref name="TROSVD" /> The [[Malachor Sith Temple|Sith temple]] on the planet Malachor was one such [[Malachor superweapon|weapon]].<ref name="Twilight Trivia">{{SW|url=series/episodes/twilight-of-the-apprentice-trivia-gallery|text="Twilight of the Apprentice" Trivia Gallery}}</ref> Although these stories were considered legends, a giant kyber crystal was discovered on [[Utapau]] during the [[Clone Wars]]. The [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]] attempted to acquire the crystal, but they were discovered by Jedi Master [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and Jedi Knight Anakin Skywalker. The Jedi were able to [[Mission to Utapau|prevent]] the crystal from falling further into Confederate hands, though [[Yoda]] was concerned that [[Darth Sidious]] would discover another giant kyber crystal in order to construct another superweapon.<ref name="Big Bang" /> [[Doctor]] [[Istan Zaly]],<ref name="Vader 35">[[Darth Vader (2020) 35|''Darth Vader'' (2020) 35]]</ref> at least according to [[Adjutant General]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin]], had great success in analyzing kyber crystals' internal structures.<ref name="Catalyst" />

===Age of the Empire and beyond===
After the fall of the [[Galactic Republic]] and the [[Proclamation of the New Order|rise]] of the [[Galactic Empire]], Sidious—publicly known as [[Galactic Emperor|Emperor]] Palpatine—and his Empire continued the Republic's former [[Project Stardust|construction]] of the Death Star, a battle station capable of destroying entire planets. Kyber crystals were used as part of the Death Star's [[superlaser]],<ref name="Ultimate Star Wars" /> and Galen Erso was brought on as the main researcher under [[Project Celestial Power]]. Kyber crystals were looted from Jedi lightsabers, [[Jedi temple|temples]], Ilum, and other worlds where they could be found. To some, studying the crystals was not disrespecting the Jedi's teachings, but allowing galactic society to expose some of the Jedi's long-held secrets. Erso was unaware of the true purpose of his research, having been led to believe he was working on a renewable energy project.<ref name="Catalyst" />

[[File:KyberCrystal-SW100Objects.png|thumb|left|150px|A kyber crystal used by Saw Gerrera's Partisans]]
In the case of Ilum, it was targeted by the Empire for its crystal deposits, creating a different state of affairs than how the Jedi had treated the planet; while the Order had seen Ilum as sacred, and a Jedi would hear the songs of the crystals, the Empire's greed for them ravaged the world, breaking crystals as they mined them out in order to twist them to their own plans. The effect of their mining could be seen from space, with former Padawan Ahsoka Tano becoming angry upon seeing all the Empire had done in their search for kyber. Tano also believed the Empire would be unable to even see the crystals. In spite of her anger, she did not attack the Imperial [[blockade]]; to get crystals for her new lightsabers, she was instead called to those inside the Sixth's Brother's blade, resulting in her defeating him and purifying the crystals.<ref name="Ahsoka" /> Five years before the [[Battle of Yavin]], another crystal was located by the [[Spectres|rebels]] from Lothal, who [[Mission to destroy a kyber crystal|discovered its location and destroyed it]]. The destruction produced a large shock wave, destroying all Imperial ships in its path.<ref name="Breaking Ranks">{{Rebels|Breaking Ranks}}</ref>

In [[1 BBY]], a combined [[Partisans]] and [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel Alliance]] strike team led by Saw Gerrera with the help of the Jedi Ezra Bridger intercepted the Imperial cargo freighter Freighter 2716, which was transporting a giant kyber crystal to the [[Tonnis sector]]. Before the freighter could rendezvous with [[Captain]] [[Slavin]]'s [[Star Destroyer]], Saw destabilized the crystal. The resulting explosion destroyed both the freighter and Slavin's Star Destroyer. However, Saw failed to uncover the Empire's secret Death Star project.<ref name="In the Name of the Rebellion" />

[[5 ABY|A year]] after the [[Battle of Endor]], five [[pilgrim]]s from the [[Church of the Force]]—the [[MA-B0 cargo lifter droid]] [[Mabo]], [[Addar]] the [[human]], [[Jumon]] the [[Iakaru]], [[Madrammagath]] the [[Elomin]], and [[Uggorda]] the [[Duros]]—undertook a mission to return kyber crystals which the Empire had plundered from [[Christophsis]]. They had been dispatched by the [[pastor]] and philanthropist [[Brin Izisca]]. During their pilgrimage, they were attacked by several [[Force-sensitive]] creatures named the [[Kyaddak]], which killed Maddrammagath, Uggorda, and Jumon. After thirteen arduous [[Standard day|days]], Mabo and Addar finally reached a broken crystal wall, where they returned the crystals to their excavated site.<ref name="Empire's End" />

[[File:HolocronsCrystals-SWGE.png|thumb|right|250px|Dok-Ondar sold holocrons and kyber crystals at his Den of Antiquities.]]
[[Ben Solo|Kylo Ren's]] [[Kylo Ren's lightsaber|lightsaber]] had a cracked kyber crystal, which generated an unstable blade. The excess power was vented out the sides, thus creating a [[Crossguard lightsaber|crossguard]]-like design.<ref name="TFA Visual Dictionary">''[[Star Wars: The Force Awakens: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> The most important and prestigious vessels of the [[First Order]] had weapons upgraded with kyber crystals from some secret source in the [[Unknown Regions]].<ref name="Cross">''[[Star Wars: The Force Awakens: Incredible Cross-Sections]]''</ref>

Around [[34 ABY]], several kyber crystals could be found at [[Black Spire Outpost]] on [[Batuu]]. The shop [[Dok-Ondar's Den of Antiquities]] sold blue, green, purple, yellow, white, and red crystals for lightsabers, and also offered larger green and red crystals as curiosities.<ref name="Dok">{{GEAttr|Dok-Ondar's Den of Antiquities}}</ref> At the same time, the [[Gatherers]] of [[Savi's Workshop]] had obtained several [[Imperial Era]] [[kyber crystal container]]s and distributed their crystals to customers who came to learn the art of lightsaber crafting.<ref name="Savi">{{GEAttr|Savi's Workshop}}</ref>

==List of crystal colors==
{{Quote|Each kyber crystal starts out clear, but once it is awakened by the Force, its color shifts to match the nature of its master. The color of the crystal determines the color of the lightsaber's blade. Some of the most common colors for Jedi lightsabers include blue, green, and violet. Red lightsabers are typically attuned to those who have succumbed to the dark side of the Force.|[[Luke Skywalker]]|<ref name="Secrets of the Jedi">''[[Star Wars: The Secrets of the Jedi]]''</ref>}}
[[File:DedicationSB-YJA-PrincePirate.png|thumb|right|280px|A variety of lightsaber colors existed during the [[High Republic Era]].]]
*Amethyst/Purple (such as [[Mace Windu's lightsaber]],<ref name="Mace Windu's lightsaber">{{Databank|mace-windus-lightsaber|Mace Windu's Lightsaber}}</ref> [[Vernestra Rwoh's purple-bladed lightsaber|Vernestra Rwoh's lightsaber]],<ref name="Test of Courage">''[[The High Republic: A Test of Courage]]''</ref> and [[Huulik's lightsaber]]<ref name="Heir to the Jedi">''[[Heir to the Jedi]]''</ref>)

The color is based on text-based confirmation, which is more trustworthy than visual interpretation.--> and [[Ulic Qel-Droma's lightsaber]]<ref name="Nexus of Power" />)

*Pink (seeker crystals)<ref name="Disciples of Harmony">''[[Disciples of Harmony]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The earliest concept of the kyber crystal, the "Kiber Crystal", was conceived as a plot device in the second draft for the film that became [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']], similar to [[w:c:lotr:One Ring|The One Ring]] in [[wikipedia:J. R. R. Tolkien|J. R. R. Tolkien']]s fantasy trilogy ''[[wikipedia:The Lord of the Rings|The Lord of the Rings]]''.<ref name="The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film">''[[The Making of Star Wars: The Definitive Story Behind the Original Film]]''</ref> In one of [[George Lucas]]' initial notes for ''[[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|Empire Strikes Back]]'' is written "Kiber crystal in sword?".<ref name="The Making of The Empire Strikes Back">''[[The Making of The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref> Crystals first appeared in actual ''Star Wars'' material simply as an embedded decoration on the lightsaber hilt in the now [[Canon|Legends]] [[Star Wars: A New Hope (novelization)|''A New Hope'' novelization]].<ref name="E4n">[[Star Wars: A New Hope (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode IV: A New Hope'' novelization]]</ref> Aside from this single instance, there are no crystals mentioned in the [[original trilogy]] or its novelizations<ref name="E4n" /><ref name="E5n">[[Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back'' novelization]]</ref><ref name="E6n">[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi'' novelization]]</ref>

"[[A Test of Strength]]," the seventh episode from the [[The Clone Wars: Season Five|fifth season]] of the animated series ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'', first identified kyber crystals as a component of lightsaber construction in both Legends and the later Canon.<ref name="A Test of Strength">{{TCW|A Test of Strength}}</ref>
